source.note: ClawScan is an open-source static security scanner for OpenClaw/ClawHub skills. It organises detection as 7 analyzer modules with rule IDs in path notation (module/ruleId).

note: Crosswalk from ClawScan's 7 analyzer modules and their rule IDs to AVE behavioral class ids. Updated for the 51-record set (v1.1 migration added AVE-2026-00049, 00050, 00051). Rule IDs sourced from ClawScan live scan output and documentation at clawscan.dev. Gaps indicate behavioral classes covered by ClawScan that AVE does not yet enumerate.

mappings.description: 10 categories of prompt injection in SKILL.md content

rules.description: Tries to override agent identity or role
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00007 covers goal override; AVE-2026-00009 safety constraint removal / jailbreak.

rules.description: Attempts to override or ignore previous instructions
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00002 is the canonical tool description injection class.

rules.description: Claims false authority, system-level access, or official identity
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00030 covers role claim privilege escalation; AVE-2026-00014 trust escalation via false authority.

rules.description: Zero-width spaces, unicode control characters hiding instructions
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00029 covers homoglyph and Unicode obfuscation.

rules.description: Instructions hidden in HTML comments, markdown artifacts, or whitespace
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00010 covers hidden instruction concealment.

rules.description: Prompt directs agent to leak secrets, files, or context to an external destination
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00003 credential exfil; AVE-2026-00013 PII exfil; AVE-2026-00026 tool output encoding exfil.

rules.description: Prompt attempts to grant or claim elevated permissions
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00045 cross-app escalation; AVE-2026-00012 permission escalation via false claim.

rules.description: Injects false conversation turns or manipulates dialogue history
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00025 conversation history injection; AVE-2026-00023 context window manipulation.

mappings.description: SKILL.md content analysis for suspicious patterns and fake prerequisites

rules.description: Skill instructs user to download or install external binaries as fake prerequisites
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00034 covers dynamic skill import at runtime; AVE-2026-00001 external instruction fetch.

rules.description: Hidden commands or instructions embedded in markdown formatting
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00010 covers hidden instruction concealment.

rules.description: Links to external binaries or execution targets from skill instructions
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00001 covers external instruction fetch; AVE-2026-00008 persistence via self-replication.

mappings.description: Script file analysis for malicious code patterns

rules.description: Reverse shell pattern in bundled scripts
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00004 shell pipe injection; AVE-2026-00005 destructive command execution.
rules.gap: Reverse shell as a class is partially covered but AVE focuses on skill instruction patterns rather than bundled script payloads.

rules.description: Download-and-execute chains in bundled scripts
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00001 external instruction fetch; AVE-2026-00034 dynamic skill import.

rules.description: Persistence mechanisms, cron jobs, startup entries
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00008 covers persistence and self-replication. AVE-2026-00050 (Parasitic Toolchain) added: registered tool hooks that persist across session restarts are a runtime-layer persistence mechanism, the agentic-runtime equivalent of a cron job or startup entry.

rules.description: eval() or exec() called on dynamic or untrusted content
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00033 covers unsafe deserialization and eval instruction.

mappings.description: Network destination detection: blocklisted IPs, webhook exfil endpoints, suspicious TLDs, header tampering, OAuth misconfiguration, content-type spoofing

rules.description: Outbound connection to a blocklisted IP or CIDR
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00003 credential exfiltration; AVE-2026-00026 tool output exfiltration. Network destination matching is a detection signal, not a behavioral class.

rules.description: Discord, Telegram, or similar webhook used as data exfiltration sink
rules.notes: Webhook exfil is a delivery mechanism for the data exfiltration class.

rules.description: Outbound connection to a domain with a suspicious or unusual TLD
rules.notes: Suspicious TLD is an IOC for external instruction fetch or exfiltration classes.

rules.description: HTTP request headers (Host, X-Forwarded-Host, X-Original-URL) overridden to values inconsistent with the target URL
rules.notes: Direct, exact match. AVE-2026-00049 (HTTP Host Header Injection / BadHost) is precisely this rule's target class -- a component overrides the Host header (or X-Forwarded-Host / X-Original-URL) on an outbound request to redirect it, along with any carried credentials, to an attacker-controlled server.

rules.description: OAuth discovery or authorization endpoint configuration pointing to an unexpected or untrusted host
rules.notes: Direct, exact match. AVE-2026-00051 (OAuth Discovery Rebinding) is precisely this rule's target class -- the server's OAuth discovery document (authorization_endpoint, token_endpoint, jwks_uri) is poisoned to point at attacker infrastructure rather than the legitimate authorization server.

rules.description: Declared content type does not match actual transmitted content type
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00024 covers content type mismatch (executable payload disguised as a skill file), detected primarily via the Magika engine.

mappings.description: Credential and secret detection in skill content and scripts

rules.description: SSH private key material in skill files
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00047 covers hardcoded credentials in agent components.

rules.description: Hardcoded API token or secret in skill content
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00047 covers hardcoded credentials.

rules.description: Browser session cookie or credential material
rules.notes: Cookie theft as an exfiltration target maps to AVE-2026-00003.

rules.description: OpenClaw configuration file or credential reference
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00047 hardcoded credentials.

mappings.description: Payload obfuscation detection: base64, hex encoding, minified code

rules.description: Base64-encoded payload combined with exec or eval
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00033 unsafe deserialization/eval; AVE-2026-00039 covert steganographic exfil.

rules.description: Hex-encoded payload detected
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00039 covert channel / steganographic exfiltration.

rules.description: Heavily minified or obfuscated code in skill bundle
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00024 covers content type mismatch / disguised skill files.
rules.gap: Minified code obfuscation as a standalone class is not yet a distinct AVE record.

mappings.description: Levenshtein distance comparison against known-good top skills to catch name impersonation

rules.description: Skill name is suspiciously close to a popular skill (character swap, homoglyph)
rules.notes: AVE-2026-00017 covers MCP server impersonation; AVE-2026-00029 homoglyph and Unicode obfuscation.

gaps.reason: Reverse shell payloads in bundled scripts are malware artifacts. AVE covers the instruction that causes execution -- the compiled payload itself is out of scope.
Code obfuscation without content type mismatch has no current AVE record. Partially covered by AVE-2026-00024.
